title: Review and setlist: Courtney Barnett shows that being a ‘creature of habit’ isn’t always a bad thing
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Courtney Barnett performed at Roadrunner in Boston to promote her album 'Creature of Habit' on May 13.
The concert featured a mix of new tracks like 'Stay in Your Lane' and hits like 'Avant Gardener' and 'Depreston'.
Brooklyn-based band Momma and West Coast musician Truman Sinclair opened the show for Barnett.

title: MassGOP-backed lieutenant governor candidate fails to collect enough signatures to make ballot
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Anne Brensley, GOP-endorsed candidate for lieutenant governor, failed to collect 10,000 signatures required for ballot qualification.
Brensley's campaign cited forgery concerns with paid signature gatherer Joe Bronske, who claimed to gather 6,203 signatures.
Brensley will run as a write-in candidate if not on ballot; deadline extensions not possible, per Secretary of State's office.

title: 7 takeaways from Patriots’ 2026 schedule
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots face eight 2025 playoff teams, including a Week 1 Super Bowl rematch against the Seahawks on September 9.
New England travels to Germany on November 15 for a game against the Lions, marking their sixth international match.
The 2026 schedule includes six nationally televised games and significant travel, totaling 27,980 miles, fifth most in the NFL.

title: Here’s your chance to design a new Boston Public Library card
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The Boston Public Library is hosting a card design contest from May 15 to June 15.
Three age groups will feature winners: youth (0-12), teens (13-17), and adults (18+).
Designs must convey the library's message, be original, and AI-generated work is prohibited.

title: ‘This was my baby’: Blaze destroys popular Belmont ice cream shop, causes floor to collapse
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A three-alarm fire destroyed Moozy's Ice Cream Emporium in Belmont early Thursday, causing the floor to collapse.
The fire started in the basement, and a ruptured gas line led to a secondary fire, officials reported.
Moozy's, a community favorite since 2012, will be demolished due to extensive damage; the fire's cause is under investigation.

title: Patriots officially unveil 2026 schedule that begins with Super Bowl rematch
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots will open the 2026 NFL season with a rematch against the Seattle Seahawks on September 9.
New England's schedule includes six nationally televised games and an international game in Munich against the Detroit Lions.
The 2026 schedule features games against eight teams that made the 2025 playoffs, including several primetime matchups.

title: Did my vehicle miss out on an important safety recall?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2009 VW Jetta owner missed a 2017 recall for an ABS module update, despite needing replacement.
Car recalls may target specific production runs; contact Volkswagen and NHTSA for potential reimbursement.
For recall issues, contact VW at 1-800-893-5298 and NHTSA's Vehicle Safety Hotline at 1-888-327-4236.

title: Bruins assistant GM leaving organization to ‘pursue other opportunities’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Assistant GM Jamie Langenbrunner leaves the Bruins after 11 years to pursue other NHL opportunities.
Langenbrunner was promoted to assistant GM in 2022 and managed player personnel and acquisition.
Potential replacements include Adam McQuaid or Zdeno Chara for Langenbrunner's position in Bruins' front office.

title: What Sam Kennedy said of Jason Varitek’s future with the Red Sox
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jason Varitek remains with the Red Sox in an undetermined role after Alex Cora and coaches were fired in April.
Red Sox President Sam Kennedy praised Varitek, stating the team will give him time to choose his future path.
Reports suggest Varitek may not want to stay with the team, but he hasn't publicly commented on his decision.

title: Former Celtics star Isaiah Thomas hired by Boston as a pro, college scout
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Isaiah Thomas hired by the Boston Celtics as a pro and college scout at 37 years old.
Thomas will be based in Seattle, assisting the Celtics with talent evaluation on the West Coast.
He joined Celtics executives at the NBA Combine in Chicago to interview draft prospects.
